question_text: A motor receives 500 J of electrical energy. It produces 350 J of mechanical energy and 150 J of heat energy. Explain why the heat energy is produced and calculate the efficiency of the motor.
correct_answer: Heat is produced due to friction in the motor's moving parts. Efficiency = 350 ÷ 500 × 100% = 70%.
explanation: Not all electrical energy converts to useful mechanical energy because friction between moving parts generates heat. Using the efficiency formula: 350 J useful ÷ 500 J total × 100% = 70%. This is a realistic efficiency for a motor.
